latest.title: Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 17:13
RNS Number : 0047V
Atlas Metals Group PLC
02 March 2026
2 March 2026
Atlas Metals Group plc
("
Atlas Metals
" or the "
Company
")
Total Voting Rights
Atlas Metals (LSE: AMG), the natural resources and energy company
,
in conformity with the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rule 5.6.1R, announces that, as at 27 February 2026, its issued share capital comprised 30,532,930
ordinary shares of nominal value £0.01 each ("
Ordinary Shares
"). Each Ordinary Share carries the right to one vote in relation to all circumstances at general meetings of the Company. The Company does not hold any shares in treasury and therefore the total number of voting rights attributable to the Ordinary Shares is 30,532,930.
The above figure of 30,532,930 may be used by shareholders of the Company as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
- Ends -
For further information, please contact:
Atlas Metals Group plc:

latest.title: Issue of Equity
latest.content:
Today 15:18
RNS Number : 7222T
Atlas Metals Group PLC
19 February 2026
19 February 2026
Atlas Metals Group plc
("
Atlas Metals
" or the "
Company
")
Issue of equity
Atlas Metals (LON: AMG), the natural resources and energy company announces that, further to the Company's circular dated 5 November 2025 and the authorities approved by shareholders of the Company at the general meeting held on 1 December 2025, application has been made to London Stock Exchange plc (the "
London Stock Exchange
") for the admission of 1,408,000 ordinary shares of nominal value £0.01 each ("
Ordinary Shares
") to be admitted to trading on the main market for listed securities of the London Stock Exchange ("
Admission
").
Admission relates to the allotment of 1,408,000 new Ordinary Shares ("
New Shares
") to be issued in terms of a historic settlement agreement.
Admission of the New Shares is expected to occur on 20 February 2026. The New Shares will rank
pari passu
in all respects with the existing Ordinary Shares.
Following Admission, the Company's issued ordinary share capital will comprise 30,532,930 Ordinary Shares, each carrying one vote. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury. The total number of voting rights in the Company following Admission will therefore be 30,532,930. With effect from Admission, this figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they determine whether they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

latest.title: Update on Proposed Acquisition
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 0418T
Atlas Metals Group PLC
16 February 2026
16 February 2026
Atlas Metals Group plc
("
Atlas Metals
" or the "
Company
")
Update on Proposed Acquisition
Atlas Metals (LON: AMG), the natural resources and energy company is pleased to provide an update
on the Company's proposed acquisition of Universal Pozzolanic Silica Alumina Ltd ("
UPSA
") (the "
Proposed Acquisition
").
As detailed in the Company's announcement on 22 December 2025, the Company engaged SLR Consulting Australia Pty Ltd ("
SLR
"), to conduct a confirmatory drilling campaign, commencing on Lots 7 and 8 at the Yammacoona Sand Quarry in New South Wales, Australia, with the intention of proving a measured resource, and a technical memorandum has been provided by SLR. A total of 26 drillholes have now been completed using three drilling methods, diamond, percussion and blast hole, for a total of 1,265 metres.
From the samples collected during the drilling programme Quantitative X-Ray Diffraction (QXRD) and clay mineralogical analyses will be undertaken to characterise material properties relevant to assessing pozzolanic potential. Drill core pieces representing all lithological units were also selected for petrographic examination and X-ray Fluorescence (XRF) analysis to document mineralogical composition and textural features.
Initial volumes pending validation of drilling data, interpretation, geological modelling and material test results within Lots 7 and 8 indicate that the concept pit (see figure 1 below) covered by the areas of Lots 7 and 8 contains a total of
86.5 million tonnes of pozzolanic sandstone from a total volume of 151.75 million tonnes of material. Whilst these initial volumes are being assessed for resource status pending material testing and validation, the purpose of the update is to increase confidence in the volumes towards measured status. Volumes may vary based on further development of the geological model and validation.
Atlas Metals is expecting SLR to complete the updated Competent Person's Report ("
CPR
"), using data obtained from this drilling programme, which is expected to include certain JORC resources being upgraded from the inferred to measured category, prior to the completion of the Proposed Acquisition. The last published CPR on the Yammacoona Sand Quarry, as announced by the Company on 28 November 2025, may be found on the Company's website at:
https://atlasmetalsgroup.com/investors/reports
The Proposed Acquisition continues to progress in line with the Board's expectations and further announcements on the progress of the Proposed Acquisition will be made when appropriate.
Figure 1 - Concept Pit Design, showing the locations of blocks 7 and 8 (from SLR CPR 26 November 2025
Illustration: Recent SLR drilling on behalf of the Company at the Yammacoona Sand Quarry in New South Wales, Australia

company_30s.what_they_do: AMG Ltd. is a mining and exploration firm focused on uranium extraction, primarily from a licensed site in Kyrgyzstan. The company also looks to acquire complementary assets to broaden its resource base.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Uranium is a critical component of the global nuclear energy mix, and demand is set to rise as countries seek low‑carbon power sources. AMG’s asset sits in a region rich in uranium, giving it a foothold in a market that is both strategic and growing.
company_30s.current_state: The business is still in a development phase, raising capital through equity and exploring acquisition opportunities. Cash flow is limited and the company is heavily reliant on financing to move the Kyrgyz project forward, while the share price has fallen sharply after a series of funding announcements.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy with a string of regulatory filings: it’s updating on a proposed acquisition that could give it a larger asset base, detailing its voting rights and holdings to reassure shareholders, and announcing new equity and warrant issuances to shore up liquidity.
A second thread focuses on the Kyrgyz uranium asset, where the latest update confirms that the licensing process is progressing and that the company is preparing for a production ramp‑up.
The third theme centers on financial structuring – an ATM facility and share‑issue plans that aim to provide short‑term cash and improve the balance sheet.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but cautious – the community is listening for a big move, but no one is rallying yet.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders believe the acquisition and the new equity deal will unlock value and that the uranium asset is a long‑term play.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others fear that the repeated equity issuances dilute shareholders and that the Kyrgyz project may face regulatory delays, keeping the price stuck.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has been flat so far; recently the chatter shifted toward a more hopeful outlook after the acquisition update, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no recent spikes in search volume, which means the market is largely unaware of AMG’s developments.

near_term_catalysts: The formal announcement of the acquisition, if it moves forward, would signal that AMG is consolidating a larger asset base and could reduce perceived risk.
Completion of the licensing process in Kyrgyzstan – a public statement confirming that the mine can start production would be a strong catalyst.
A significant uptick in trading volume, especially on a day when the share price touches a key support level, could indicate that institutional buyers are stepping in.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to monitor over the next two months include: (1) any formal agreement or due‑diligence report on the acquisition; (2) a regulatory approval letter for the Kyrgyz project; (3) a spike in daily volume that exceeds the 20‑day moving average by at least 30%; (4) a shift in the company’s debt profile or a new financing tranche that improves liquidity.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a capitulation cycle – the price is still far below its historical peak, and the APEX score suggests a tight range that could snap once a catalyst arrives.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the acquisition goes through and the Kyrgyz license is granted, the company will have a larger, more secure asset base. A deep crash of this magnitude historically offers a 2‑ to 3‑fold upside if the price recovers to mid‑range levels, giving a 200‑300% return on the current low.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The risk lies in the company’s heavy reliance on new equity, which could dilute shareholders further and signal ongoing cash‑flow problems. If the acquisition stalls or the Kyrgyz license faces delays, the stock could remain mired in a low‑range squeeze, potentially falling another 20‑30% before any rebound.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the current low price (the worst‑case is a further 20% dip). Potential gain is a 2‑ to 3‑fold upside if the price reaches the mid‑range. The asymmetry is roughly 1:2.5 to 1:3.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the share price breaks above the 20‑day moving average with volume 1.5× the 20‑day average. Size the position so that a 20% drop would hit your stop, and trail the stop to lock in gains as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until you see a clear acquisition announcement or a regulatory approval for the Kyrgyz asset, or until daily volume spikes 30% above the 20‑day average on a support level.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy protection or the stock is delisted, exit immediately. Otherwise, stay bullish on the deep‑crash setup with a clear catalyst.

ai_insights.bottom_line: AMG is a deep‑crashed uranium play that could double or triple in value once an acquisition or licensing milestone clears, but it remains vulnerable to dilution and regulatory delays.
